text.tspan: PROBLEM: this query is expensive (joins 5 tables, aggregates 100M rows, runs 30 seconds).
It runs 10,000 times per minute. Most of the underlying data doesn't change minute-to-minute.
WHAT YOU WANT: compute the answer ONCE, store it, serve it cheaply.
MATERIALIZED VIEW: a stored, pre-computed result
Reads are fast (just a lookup); writes drift the underlying data and the view must be refreshed.
1. FULL REFRESH: rebuild the entire view on a schedule (every N minutes)
+ simple, correct, predictable
u2212 view is stale between refreshes; large views are slow to rebuild
2. INCREMENTAL REFRESH: only update affected rows when source changes
+ much faster, near-real-time
u2212 complex to implement; depends on tracking source-data changes (CDC, triggers)
3. ON-DEMAND: compute only when queried, cache result
+ bounded cost per query
u2212 first query is slow; cache invalidation is tricky
4. EVENT-DRIVEN: source-system events trigger view updates
+ near-real-time; explicit, observable
u2212 requires event infrastructure (Kafka, CDC)
u2022 In the SAME DB (Postgres MATERIALIZED VIEW, Oracle, SQL Server indexed views)
u2022 In a SECONDARY DB tuned for reads (read replica, Elasticsearch, columnar store)
u2022 In a CACHE layer fed by events
u2022 In a STREAM PROCESSOR (Kafka Streams, Flink, ksqlDB) writing to a serving store
u2022 In a SEPARATE READ MODEL (CQRS pattern)
